old_claim.note: Drop Spindles provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No spindle-whorl dependency remains because spinning and warp tension are separate textile operations, and the source explicitly tracks their artifacts as distinct technologies.

source_shape.source_locator: Ulucak discussion states that spindle whorls appear earlier and loom weights emerge later around 6000 BCE, treating the two assemblages separately.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Spindle whorls produce yarn while loom weights tension warp; co-occurrence in textile production does not make one artifact a component of the other.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source's separate chronology and functions support sibling components feeding weaving, not a direct spindle-to-weight causal edge.

ontology_before: A warp-tension component depended directly on an unrelated yarn-spinning component.
ontology_after: Spindle whorls and loom weights remain distinct textile tools connected through downstream production contexts.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: loom_weights no longer directly depends on drop_spindles.
would_reject_if: Evidence showed that the Ulucak weights were manufactured from or mechanically operated by spindle whorls.
short_reason: Spinning whorls and loom weights perform separate operations.
